sections.heading: SCOPE OF THE CODE
sections.summary: The Code of Business Conduct is part of the Fair Market Conduct Programme implemented by the Financial Services Commission (FSC). It establishes 9 Guiding Principles that define licensees' obligations for sound business conduct in the financial services sector. The objectives include ensuring sound conduct, building consumer confidence, maintaining Mauritius' reputation as an international financial centre, and ensuring fair treatment of consumers and the public.

requirements.text: The Code of Business Conduct (the "Code") is part of the Fair Market Conduct Programme which the Financial Services Commission (the "Commission") is implementing.
The Code has 9 Guiding Principles, which set out in general terms a licensee's obligations to ensure the sound conduct of business.

The objectives of the Code are to ensure sound conduct of business in the financial services sector; instil confidence in the financial services sector; establish standards in order to preserve and maintain the good repute of Mauritius as an international financial centre; and ensure fair treatment of consumers of financial services and the public in general.

Some of the Guiding Principles are already embedded in existing legislative provisions or market practices. The onus is on the licensee to adopt appropriate processes and procedures, in line with their business model, to meet their obligations under the Guiding Principles.

sections.source_quote: The Code of Business Conduct (the "Code") is part of the Fair Market Conduct Programme which the Financial Services Commission (the "Commission") is implementing.

sections.heading: EFFECTIVE DATE
sections.summary: The Code of Business Conduct comes into effect on 01 January 2016. This section establishes the effective date for the implementation of the Code, which is a key milestone for financial institutions operating under the Fair Market Conduct Programme.

requirements.text: The Code shall come into effect on 01 January 2016.

sections.source_quote: The Code shall come into effect on 01 January 2016.

sections.heading: explanatory notes have been included.
sections.summary: This section details 9 Guiding Principles for financial licensees, including requirements for skill and care in customer interactions, business integrity, conflict of interest management, customer communication, asset protection, compliance with regulations, financial resource management, internal affairs, and cooperation with the Financial Services Commission. Each principle includes specific obligations and explanatory notes.

requirements.text: Licensees must act with due skill, care and diligence towards their customers and other stakeholders.

Licensees must have in place appropriate safeguards against occurrences of behaviour that constitute a lack of due skill, care and diligence amounting to culpable negligence. These safeguards include appropriate staff training.
Licensees must apply the "fit and proper" test for their officers and persons acting on their behalf.
Licensees must observe high standards of honesty, integrity and fairness and ensure that all business transactions are carried out and recorded fairly and accurately.
This principle includes any obligation to avoid misleading and deceptive acts or representations.
Licensees must avoid situations of conflict of interests, and in case a conflict arises, licensees are required to ensure the fair treatment of all their customers.

This principle recognises that conflict of interests may be managed and that proper management to ensure fair treatment of customers may require disclosure, internal rules of confidentiality, or other appropriate methods or combinations of methods.
Licensees must seek the necessary information about their customers' individual circumstances and financial objectives.
Licensees must ensure that customers are provided with accurate, timely and comprehensible information that would enable them to make informed decisions.

This principle includes any obligation of the licensees: to know their customers and to fulfil any suitability requirements; to acquire and to provide information, including information about risks, required by the customer to make informed investment decisions; to provide timely and accurate reports to the customer about business undertaken with the customer.

Licensees must ensure that, in cases where they have control over their clients' assets, these are, at all times, properly segregated and identifiable. This includes ensuring that client's money is properly segregated.

Licensees must segregate the clients' assets which they hold from their own. The aim is to ensure that client's assets are protected from risk of loss and that they can be easily identified in case of insolvency of the licensee or the client.

Licensees must observe high standards of market conduct and must comply with all regulatory requirements applicable to the conduct of its business activities so as to promote the best interests of customers and the integrity of the market.
Licensees must ensure compliance with all applicable laws, including, but not limited to the relevant Acts, regulations, rules and guidelines issued thereunder.

Licensees must ensure that they maintain, at all times, adequate financial resources to meet their financial obligations and that they implement appropriate risk management policies in their business.

Licensees must manage their business in a responsible and sustainable manner while ensuring that adequate controls are maintained. They must also ensure that, at all times, proper systems and procedures are in place in respect of operations, record keeping, human resources, training, compliance as well as such other matters as the Commission may deem necessary.
Licensees must deal with the Commission in a co-operative manner and must comply with their disclosure obligations in a prompt manner.

sections.source_quote: Licensees must act with due skill, care and diligence towards their customers and other stakeholders.

sections.heading: COMPLIANCE WITH THE CODE
sections.summary: All financial licensees must comply with the Code of Business Conduct. The Board and other officers are responsible for setting the tone and guiding business practices to ensure employees and third parties contribute to sound business conduct. Licensees must maintain records of compliance arrangements for Commission inspection, and non-compliance may result in regulatory actions under relevant Acts.

requirements.text: All licensees must comply with the Code.

The Board and other officers of licensees are responsible for setting the tone and guiding business practices in the organisation so that all employees of the licensees and other persons acting on the behalf of the licensees can contribute to sound business conduct.
Licensees must keep appropriate records of any arrangements made to comply with the Code. These records must be available for inspection by the Commission, at any time, upon request.
Non-compliance with the Code will expose the licensee to regulatory actions in accordance with the relevant Acts.

sections.source_quote: All licensees must comply with the Code.
